description: Solution Graph. Joins g01's lecture_baseline@1 (the lecture skeleton plus claim/concept join keys) and g02's research hand-off (officially solution_input_candidate@1, legacy user_approved_research_bundle@1 still accepted) and turns them into a full plan for a NEW presentation plus a ready-to-paste generator prompt. The boundary input is a thin composite (solution_graph_input@1) of those two refs. The producer chain is: g03-a01-solution-architect grounds research findings into existing slides (solution_blueprint@1, the evidence layer); g03-a02-slide-architect critiques existing slides (KEEP/UPDATE/MERGE/SPLIT/REORDER) and proposes NEW slides interleaved between them, grounded in g02 coverage_gaps/unresolved_items/optional_improvements/topics/recommended_claims/market_case_findings (slide_plan@1); the User Change-Plan Gate approves the deck and picks the target tool; g03-a03-slide-designer designs every slot (content, layout, design and a 6-10 sentence narrative per slide: slide_design_set@1); g03-a04-prompt-builder renders the final Markdown prompt for the chosen tool via one of three tool skills (presentation_prompt@1); the User Final-Review Gate approves it. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the node sequence: g03_flow.py and the orchestrator prompt must agree with this manifest. Reviewers are runs of ONE physical agent (g03-a10-output-reviewer) with a per-node review_profile; the user gates are orchestrator steps. The primary exit is presentation_prompt@1; solution_blueprint@1, slide_plan@1 and slide_design_set@1 are persisted secondary exits.
